topic: Mosel residual sugar and low alcohol
trigger_keywords: mayor dulzor residual y menor alcohol

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Riesling from a cool Mosel site is fermented to a style that retains part of its grape sugar.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Stopping fermentation before all sugar becomes alcohol leaves residual sweetness and produces less alcohol, while high acidity balances the sugar.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Many sweet or off-dry Mosel styles combine noticeable residual sugar, low alcohol and high acidity.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: retained sugar means less sugar converted to alcohol, explaining sweetness with lower alcohol in the relevant style.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
